slides.title: Revenue quality pressure points
slides.strapline: Concentrated churn, price resets, and working-capital drag are tightening quality of revenue in FY2025.
body: Top ten customers represent 61% of recurring revenue and two enterprise contracts are approaching renewal with explicit rebid language.
Logo retention remained high at 94%, but gross dollar retention fell to 86% as legacy contracts were repriced at lower volume commitments.
Sales incentives shifted to upfront discounting in Q4, increasing ACV conversion but pulling forward margin impact into the next two quarters.
Implementation overruns on three strategic accounts required unplanned service credits and delayed milestone billing by six to eight weeks.
Base-case underwriting should ring-fence near-term revenue confidence to contracted backlog and apply explicit downside sensitivity to FY2026 growth assumptions.
slides.source: Source: CRM cohort extract, contract register, and monthly bridge pack (FY2024-FY2025).

slides.title: Risk and mitigation priorities
slides.strapline: Execution risk is manageable if commercial controls and delivery governance are tightened in the first 90 days.
leftBody: Repricing cadence is inconsistent across enterprise cohorts, creating avoidable renewal leakage and non-standard discount terms.
Pipeline staging quality is weak in two regions where close dates are repeatedly rolled without clear owner-level escalation.
Resource utilization volatility in implementation teams has increased rework and extended cash conversion on fixed-fee projects.
rightBody: Introduce approval guardrails for non-standard terms with weekly exception review at CRO and FP&A level.
Standardize renewal playbooks by cohort with mandatory deal-margin checkpoints before final quote issuance.
Deploy account-level burn monitoring and milestone gating so scope drift is surfaced before it affects billing confidence.

slides.title: Growth slowed after pricing reset
slides.strapline: Revenue growth decelerated after the FY2024 price reset while churn remained elevated in lower-margin cohorts.
body: Quarterly revenue growth declined from 18% to 9% across the period, with the steepest deceleration in accounts exposed to contract rebasing.
Churn rate remained above the long-run average after Q2 FY2025 despite improved new-logo bookings in mid-market channels.
Discount-heavy renewal tactics preserved volume but compressed realized price and reduced expansion contribution from existing customers.
Investment case should separate temporary pricing actions from structural demand and model a staged recovery path tied to renewal quality improvements.

chart.source: Source: Management monthly KPI pack, cohort bridge, and revenue analytics cube.
